item.title: Mad Max: Fury Road - Behind the Scenes
item.description:
it's another behind the scenes with your boy CM7.. hope you enjoy this installment which features a somewhat recent favorite. A movie that is just so cool visually that you can watch it on mute and enjoy it.. but don't do that because the sound is also amazing and won 2 oscars (of 6 total). anywho..
This is the 2nd Mad Max film to feature Hugh Keays-Byrne. He first played the villain Toecutter in Mad Max (1979), and in this installment, he plays head villain Immortan Joe.
Director/writer/producer George Miller, he has directed/written all of the Mad Max movies. This film was shot in sequence.. aka first scene -> last scene.. this is uncommon for most films
Nux's (Nicholas Hoult) make-up comprised 8 silicone prosthetics followed by airbrushing, clay application and various levels of dirt. This make-up had to be applied 77 times and took 2 hours each time.
the jacket used by Tom Hardy is a replica of the one used by Mel Gibson in his final two Mad Max films.Gibson's jacket was located in storage and copied exactly
Production was originally set to film around 2011 in the traditional setting of Broken Hill, Australia, but due to heavy rainfall altering the desert setting into a lush meadow of flowers, the production was transferred to Namibia
Hardy and Miller reportedly had a rocky relationship during shooting but have since patched things up
The flame-shooting guitarist is Australian artist/musician Sean Hape, also known as Iota. In an interview on Vice (2013), he said the guitar is 132 lbs and shot real gas-powered flames, which he controlled using the whammy bar. apparently he might be taking a nap between takes ^^
Margaret Sixel (Editor) is married to director George Miller. When Sixel asked why he thought she should take on this project, as she had never done an action film, Miller replied, "Because if a guy did it, it would look like every other action movie." Sixel's work was more than up to par; she won an Oscar for Best Editing. Sixel had around 470 hours of footage to edit. Watching it required three months.
First movie of the Mad Max franchise to be nominated for an Oscar. It won six.
The vehicle driven by villain Immortan Joe, the "Gigahorse", is fully functional, and was designed and constructed entirely for the film. the design of stacked Coupe DeVilles was intended to showcase the clout he wields. The Gigahorse features dual supercharged Chevrolet 502 V8 engines linked together by a custom gearbox feeding into an Allison transmission. In this configuration, the Gigahorse produced over 1,200 horsepower and was capable of hitting 75 mph. According to Hugh Keays-Byrne, who played Immortan Joe, the vehicle was his favorite, and could be heard for miles when it was being operated
sadly this was Hugh Keays-Byrne's final film. He passed in 2020
150 cars were manufactured for the film. only 15 are still around
The unique tracked vehicle driven by the Bullet Farmer, known as "The Peacemaker" was considered the most dangerous and difficult vehicle on set. It was constructed by mating a Ripsaw tank chassis with a Valiant Charger body. Despite extensive R&D, the vehicle still had severe problems with braking and engine cooling. The original diesel engine was replaced with a water cooled Merlin V8, which was itself replaced by a Chevrolet 502 V8 when the former was destroyed after ingesting sand on set. A complete redesign of the brake and cooling systems was required to make the vehicle usable on set. Like many of the vehicles used in the film, The Peacemaker was crushed after production concluded
According to Tom Hardy, he dined with Mel Gibson to discuss him taking over the iconic role of Max Rockatansky. Gibson told him "I'm just too old to effectively play Max" and gave Hardy his blessing. Max has only 52 lines in the entire film
Miller envisioned the film as being 1 long, epic chase scene
More than eighty percent of the effects used in the film are traditional (practical) effects, including stunts, make-up, and sets. CGI was used lightly, mainly to enhance the Namibian surroundings, remove stunt rigging, and for Imperator Furiosa's left arm, which is a prosthetic limb
To prepare themselves for combat, ready to die for Immortan Joe, the War Boys spray their lips and teeth with a silver substance, similar to common spray paint. Both the War Boys and Immortan Joe often speak about this as though it is a religious rite, saying that it will allow them to enter "the gates of Valhalla, shiny and chrome". However, in a May 2015 interview with CraveOnline, actor Hugh Keays-Byrne, who plays Immortan Joe, said that this practice, which the War Boys think is purely ritualistic, actually involves the inhalation of "...a very euphoric drug" that keeps the War Boys high and suicidally devoted to Immortan Joe. Another clue to the spray's narcotic properties is the fact that "chrome" and "chroming" are Australian slang terms for inhalant abuse that's all for now.. here's the others https://imgur.com/gallery/GuGbpN6 - Independence Day https://imgur.com/gallery/DhYcwrE - Silence of the Lambs https://imgur.com/gallery/WO6gzMM -- Raiders of the Lost Ark https://imgur.com/gallery/ZMhvuh0 - Total Recall https://imgur.com/gallery/WmOWoIZ - Lord of the Rings Trilogy https://imgur.com/gallery/8Z4kElY -- Jurassic Park https://imgur.com/gallery/3VAvdAg -- Full Metal Jacket https://imgur.com/gallery/LPF2snJ -- Saving Private Ryan https://imgur.com/gallery/CynT3Lf -- Dazed and Confused https://imgur.com/gallery/bSoP2u4 -- Gangs of New York https://imgur.com/gallery/y9Kxrfc -- The Shawshank Redemption https://imgur.com/gallery/O9SsH6n -- Beetlejuice https://imgur.com/gallery/c3amBZh - Batman (1989) https://imgur.com/gallery/1C890od -- Titanic https://imgur.com/gallery/mcoXkG5 - The Big Lebowski https://imgur.com/gallery/YiXXVgH -- Die Hard https://imgur.com/gallery/JeAgqjp - Goodfellas https://imgur.com/gallery/7fvyQYp - Predator https://imgur.com/gallery/7x0RarB - The Matrix https://imgur.com/gallery/QOhVsLI - Gladiator https://imgur.com/gallery/0JoSbTr - Robocop https://imgur.com/gallery/ZzQ3xgj - Aliens https://imgur.com/gallery/Q9gHx4d - T2 https://imgur.com/gallery/1SCGLPE - Starship Troopers

item.title: Let Night Owls Sleep!
item.description: Just want to vent and let this die in usersub. I have always been a night owl. For my entire life, my body prefers sleeping from 4am-12pm. I have worked many night jobs, and they have been some of my favorites. I'm just tired of some of my friends acting like I'm whining when they wake me up at 8am. I understand that they sleep from 10pm to 6am, but just because I'm not awake at 8am does not mean I'm being lazy or "sleeping in". Our whole society is geared towards early morning people, and it sucks.
item.dc:creator: Mynamedoesntmatteronlylifematters

item.description: Don't really care if this dies in user sub I just need to scream into the void. Can't just quit I really need to have some form of income. item.title: Penn Jillette on not being a libertarian any more
item.description: Seems reasonable to me. Other interesting bits in the article too, including his very low opinion of Trump. https://www.cracked.com/amp/article_40871_penn-jillette-wants-to-talk-it-all-out.html FP edit: thanks! And I should've said that I think this is great of Jillette. Tons of people don't change their minds, especially about something they've said publicly. Those that do often claim they haven't changed, or don't publicly admit their mistake, or waste so much time explaining why their mistake was justified that they're practically still arguing for their mistaken position. So I applaud him saying "I need to be very ashamed of [my old position]," and I hope I can manage the same with my own errors.
item.dc:creator: wingineer

item.title: Bottom shelf Thursday dump ;)
item.description:
@HileyNoteson
@EvilCatCreations @KibblesandBitchTits
@jimfalconer611 @supervillainsevilmoustache @ImayHaveThePerfectComment
@perkamoose
@Opieone5
@BadBoyBoris @idonotthinkitownswhatyouthinkitowns @Imakethingsweird @MrGinn
@HorrorMango
@MosquitoHawker @tarnok @TransFlavoredDepression
You can catch me live on twitch.tv/bottomshelfvodka streaming with my favorite Imgurians from the Bottom Shelf Community server.
Join for the games! We've been playing Lethal Company, Red Dead Redemption 2, Pay Day 3, GTA V, Jackbox, Among Us, 7 Days to Die, Sea of Thieves, Barotrauma, Minecraft, Ultimate Chicken Horse...if there's a specific game you like, I guarantee you'll find a shitposting meme loving Imgurian there to play it with you. Join for the memes! Imgurians share memes in the meme dumpster 24/7 Join for the Awe! Pay your pet taxes in the pet tex channel Join for the NSFW memes! Join to chat with hilarious people! Join to lurk in the corners! Join for the shockingly magnificent balance of wholesomeness and chaos. discord.gg/bottomshelfvodka Still unsure about the server? Check out our museum of stupidity. https://imgur.com/gallery/FYpqqAf
#Taco Supreme With The Sour Cream Mustache tax.
item.dc:creator: BottomShelfVodka

item.title: A sh*t storm in the Colorado Rockies
item.description:
Hello friends. This is our current situation in Park County, Colorado. Yes, the Park County that inspired South Park. This is a site in the Rockies uphill and less than 1/4 mile from numerous residences (as seen in the top left of the image here) that is still zoned as residential and agricultural. We have a South Park worthy case of a community business man running roughshod over our community and representatives who seem eager to allow this person to destroy our environment, our well water, and our general quality of life.
Even before receiving proper permitting and evaluation https://www.shirleyseptic.com/ has decided to full steam ahead with a waste transfer station they have decided to place in one of the least appropriate places. They claim everything is up to code to prevent the escape of harmful gasses and odors that can impact our community or combust and start a fire, and of course you can clearly see that care here.
Even though there will/is certainly an environmental impact to our native deer and elk it couldn't be that bad, right?
We've fortunately managed to get bits of traction with the news, but oddly enough posts discussing concerns get deleted from local NextDoor and Facebook pages. https://www.denver7.com/news/investigations/park-county-leaders-accused-of-giving-preferential-treatment-to-businessman https://ourcommunitynow.com/news-local/park-county-rezoning-project-preferential-treatment?fbclid=IwAR1oIDMllRE4n5TfUgsUlsW0iIZurpkC6Te5xfG31ymGrUdf0SfPbVdEBK4
I'm sure it helps that the company has been around for years, and for quite some time they've been allowed to work without license plates or registration on any of their commercial vehicles. They've been allowed to transport and dump both trash and human waste without reprisal.
They claim that there are only a couple of empty storage units stored on the property, and the building that received retroactive approval.
We love our homes, and know we are very fortunate to live in such a beautiful place. We only want our water to stay clean and safe and not to lose our homes and lives to fire.
Here's a fire that popped up at Reffel Ranch in Jefferson County, which borders Park County. This site is where Shirley Septic last dumped human waste illegally, and where the EPA finally stepped in and requested cleanup. There is still sludge on the property as well as "empty" storage containers. If you could take a moment to give the news articles a click or even send and email of your thoughts to our commissioners we would appreciate it more than you know. Amy Mitchell (Chair) amitchell@parkco.us Dick Elsner delsner@parkco.us Dave Wissel dwissel@parkco.us
item.dc:creator: Painahimah
